en.extract:
The rufous-sided honeyeater (Ptiloprora erythropleura) is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae.
It is endemic to West Papua, Indonesia. There are currently 2 recognized subspecies.
Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.


Description
Rufous-sided honeyeaters generally weigh from 18-25 grams. Their head and necks are a greyish color, while under the wings is a more chestnut-brown color. Not much is known about juvenile coloring.


Diet and behavior
The birds are omnivorous, eating insects, nectar, and fruit. They will forage from all levels of vegetation, but mostly the middle and upper levels.
Often the birds are seen in pairs, or in small groups.
Their sound is made of rapid "plaintive whistled 'chwee' notes", although this varies by location.

The '''rufous-sided honeyeater''' ('''''Ptiloprora erythropleura''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Meliphagidae]].
It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[West Papua (region)|West Papua]], [[Indonesia]]. There are currently 2 recognized subspecies.<ref name="Higgins 2020">{{Cite journal |last=Higgins |first=Peter J. |last2=Christidis |first2=Les |last3=Ford |first3=Hugh |date=2020 |title=Rufous-sided Honeyeater (Ptiloprora erythropleura), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/rushon1/1.0/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.rushon1.01 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.

== Description ==
Rufous-sided honeyeaters generally weigh from 18-25 grams. Their head and necks are a greyish color, while under the wings is a more chestnut-brown color. Not much is known about juvenile coloring.<ref name="Higgins 2020" />

== Diet and behavior ==
The birds are [[Omnivore|omnivorous]], eating insects, nectar, and fruit. They will forage from all levels of vegetation, but mostly the middle and upper levels.<ref name="Higgins 2020" />

Often the birds are seen in pairs, or in small groups.<ref name="Higgins 2020" />

Their sound is made of rapid "plaintive whistled 'chwee' notes", although this varies by location.<ref name="Higgins 2020" />
